epics.title: Restructure songs data around handwritten lead-sheet charts

tasks.title: Replace prose-first song records with chart-first song records
acceptance_criteria: The songs library data represents chart metadata, sections, systems, and measure-level chord cells instead of freeform notation strings.
The data model supports slash chords, repeats, meter changes, and short performance directives.
At least one sample song is transcribed from the provided handwritten chart structure.
done_definition: The shared song types and seed data match the lead-sheet reading workflow.

epics.title: Turn the songs workspace into a chart library and lead-sheet viewer

tasks.title: Update the song list to preview chart-relevant information
acceptance_criteria: The list emphasizes chart title, key center, feel, and compact structural metadata.
Selection state remains obvious without introducing extra primary actions.
The list still works as the persistent navigation surface for opening charts.
done_definition: The list items read like chart library entries rather than generic song cards.

tasks.title: Render the selected song like a practical lead sheet
acceptance_criteria: The detail pane renders section groups and system rows with measure-level chord cells.
Performance cues such as repeats, D.S., meter changes, and ending notes are visible next to the relevant system.
The viewer feels closer to a paper chart than to a prose metadata page.
done_definition: The selected song can be read as a functional digital chart with stable visual rhythm.

assumptions: The current MVP remains local and synchronous, so loading and fetch error states are still deferred.
Titles and artist names may remain present, but the UI hierarchy should prioritize chart structure over song-card metadata.
The provided handwritten sample is authoritative enough to seed one representative chart even if some fine-grained repeat semantics remain abbreviated.
risks: A measure grid that is too decorative could reduce legibility compared with the handwritten source.
If the chart data model is still too flat, later songs with pickups, codas, or alternate endings may require another shape change.
The handwritten sample leaves some turnaround intent implicit, so a few transcribed cues may need later adjustment.
